title: malache scabra
intro: Malache scabra, also known as Wild Potato or Indian Potato, is a plant species that belongs to the family Malpighiaceae. It is a shrub-like plant with edible tubers, traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The tubers of Malache scabra contain compounds with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to provide health benefits such as reducing blood sugar levels, aiding in digestion, and supporting immune function. Malache scabra is often prepared as a decoction, powder, or infusion for its medicinal use, and has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine systems.
